history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible at row 05 column F, straight ahead and two cells to the right. However, the cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. Given my orientation, turning right or left will allow me to navigate around the wall. Looking at the map, turning left will face me toward the open floor cells, which appears to be a safer path compared to turning right where there is also a wall. I will turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I turned left previously, now I am facing left (west). The cell directly in front is a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. The cell to the right is lava (L), which I must avoid. To navigate around this, I should turn left again to face down (south) where there might be a safer path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing down (south). The cell directly in front of me is floor (.), which is safe to move into. Moving forward will help me explore more of the map and potentially find a path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation shows no change from before moving forward, which suggests I may have not moved forward successfully. The cell in front is floor (.), so I will try to move forward once more to proceed safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation is still the same, indicating no movement forward occurred or the view has not updated. Since the cell directly in front is a wall (#) at the row 7 column D (my location is at D7), moving forward is blocked. The cells to my left (C7) and right (E7) can be considered. The left cell is floor (.) and the right cell is wall (#). I will turn left to face the floor cell at left (which is safe) and then move forward next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I have turned left and now face towards cell C7, which is floor (.). The goal is spotted at B5. The way forward seems to be going left and then moving forward and up toward the goal. From this position, the cell in front is floor (.) at C7, so I will move forward into it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I moved forward to cell C7 and now my current position is at C7 facing upwards. The goal is now at B6. The cell in front D6 is floor (.) and safe to move into. To approach the goal at B6, I need to first turn left to face B7 and then move forward. I will turn left now.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Now facing left at position C7, the cell in front B7 is floor (.) and safe to move into. Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal at E5. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
